original: Chinese mountain climber and double amputee Xia Boyu reaches the summit of Mt Everest
Stanford University scientists develop a prototype bionic eye
Robert Zemeckis, American film director (Forrest Gump, Back to the Future), born in Chicago, Illinois
Gabriel Medinabecomes the first surfer ever to land the move "Backflip" in competition
World's Largest Burrito created at 1,126 lbs
Eoin Colfer, Irish author of children's books (Artemis Fowl), born in Wexford, Ireland[1][2]
English country doctorEdward Jenneradministers his revolutionary cowpox-based vaccine for smallpox in Berkeley, Gloucestershire
Skylab launched, 1st Space Station
USSR launch 1st (unmanned) space capsule
First passenger flight in an airplane
Successful memory transfer in snails achieved by scientists from University of California published in journal "eNeuro"
NASA launches space vehicle S-192
Meriwether LewisandWilliam Clark's expedition sets out from St. Louis for the Pacific Coast, commissioned byThomas Jefferson
Netherlands Institute for War Documentation publishesAnne Frank's complete diary
Elia Dalla Costa, Italian cardinal who helped organize a rescue network for Jewish people during the Holocaust, born in Villaverla, Italy (d. 1961)
US PresidentHarry Trumansigns bill establishing a rocket test range at Cape Canaveral
The Canellas meteorite, an 859-gram chondrite-type meteorite, strikes the earth near Barcelona, Spain
England soccer team beats Nazi-Germany, 6-3
US Congress designates Carlsbad Caverns in New Mexico a National Park
English colonists establish the 1st permanent English settlement in America at Jamestown. Unknown to them they have landed amidst the worst drought in 800 years.
Illustrated London News; the world's first illustrated weekly newspaper, begins publication
Oldest continuously operating movie theater in the world, the State Theatre first opens in Washington, Iowa (Guinness World Records)
LA's Griffith Planetarium opens, 3rd in US
Underground America Day is 1st observed to honor the 6,000 Americans that make their homes in the Earth
Lowest US temperature in May recorded (-10°F /-23°C at Climax, Colorado)
Cyclone Mocha makes landfall on Rakhine coast, Myanmar, near southern border of Bangladesh with at least 400 killed. Second strongest storm on record in the North Indian Ocean.

Octavia E. Butlerreceives the first-ever Infinity Award from the Science Fiction and Fantasy Writers Association, an award given to those who have died before they could be considered for a Grand Master award
Beatles announce formation of Apple Corp
US Supreme court approves equal rights to females in military
Washington SenatorWalter Johnsonends MLB record scoreless streak at 56 innings
David Byrne, Scottish-American rock singer-songwriter (Talking Heads - "Burning Down the House"; "Psycho Killer"), born in Dumbarton Scotland
Russi Taylor, American voice actress (6th Minnie Mouse, 1986-2019), born in Cambridge, Massachusetts (d. 2019)
"The Adventures of Robin Hood", directed by Michael Curtiz and William Keighley, and starringErrol FlynnandOlivia de Havillandpremieres
"Little Shop of Horrors" is released in Germany
Stirling Mosswins the 1961 Monaco Grand Prix
Stanley Cup Final, Boston Garden, Boston, MA: Jacques Lemaire scores twice as Montreal Canadiens edge Boston Bruins, 2-1 in OT for a 4 game sweep and back-to-back titles
Jose Oquendo is the first non-pitcher in 20 years to get a decision in a baseball game as he and the St. Louis Cardinals lose to the Braves 7-5 in 19 innings
Pride of Baltimore lost at sea.
"She Blinded Me with Science" by Thomas Dolby hits #5
The National Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Children (NSPCC) is launched in London, England, led by Benjamin Waugh
US Women's Army Auxiliary Corps (WAAC) forms
Land surveyor, newspaper publisher and inventorGail Bordenpatents his process for condensed milk
Adolphe Nicole of Switzerland patents the chronograph
Delegates gather in Philadelphia to draw up the Constitution of the United States
Louis XIVbecomes King of France aged 4
Warsaw Pact is signed by the Soviet Union, Albania, Bulgaria, Czechoslovakia, East Germany, Hungary, Poland and Romania
Paraguay gains independence from Spain (National Day)
John Ware, American-Canadian black rancher and cowboy born into slavery, born in North Texas (d. 1905)[1]
William G Gregory, American USAF Major and astronaut (STS 67), born in Lockport New York
Claudia Goldin, American academic and economist (2023 Nobel Prize for Economics for work on women’s labour market outcomes), born in New York City
Tania León, Cuban-American Pulitzer Prize-winning composer (Haiku; Scourge of the Hyacinths), conductor, arts advisor, and educator, born in Havana, Cuba
Kottayam Pushpanath [Pushpanathan Pillai], Indian science fictioin and mystery novelist, known as the "Arthur Conan Doyle of India", born in Kottayam, British India (d. 2018)
Bobby Darin [Walden Robert Cassotto], American singer ("Mack the Knife"; "Splish-Splash"; "Beyond The Sea"), born in The Bronx, New York (d. 1973)
Gillian Marucha Bradshaw, American author of historical fiction, sci-fi and children's literature (Down the Long Wind, The Dragon and the Thief), born in Falls Church, Virginia
Norodom Sihamoni, King of Cambodia (2004-), former ballet dancer, and cultural ambassador, born in Phnom Penh, Cambodia[1]
Pierre Victor Auger, French physicist who is known for the discovery of giant air showers, born in Paris, France (d. 1993)[1]
Ed Ricketts, American marine biologist (Between Pacific Tides, Sea of Cortez), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1948)
Sidney Bechet, American jazz clarinetist, saxophonist, bandleader, and composer ("Petite Fleur"), born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 1959)
Sonia d’Artois, British Canadian WWII resistance agent, born in Eastchurch, Kent (d. 2014)
Eric Morecambe [John Bartholomew], British comedian (Morecambe & Wise, Picadilly Palace), born in Morecambe, Lancashire (d. 1984)
